vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is partially accurate but shows significant degradation. P3 (Pothos) is present with the anchor. P1 (String of Nickels) is present in the yellow pot. P2/P4 (Mexican Mint/Silver Guest) appear to be represented by a single, struggling specimen in the foreground black pot, indicating a potential systemic loss of the secondary species.

inventory_reconciliation.P4: Systemic Loss (No distinct specimen identified in shared pot)

plant_audit.P2: Single leaf remaining, showing signs of chlorosis and potential detachment.
plant_audit.P3: One necrotic leaf (left), one healthy leaf (right). Rabbit anchor remains stable.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Consistently damp/dark; no visible surface cracking.
biome_observations.debris: White perlite/egg-shell fragments present in P3 and P2 pots.

temporal_deltas.T-5_to_T-1: Gradual decline in P3 leaf turgor; P2 leaf shows progressive yellowing/browning.
temporal_deltas.T-1_to_CURRENT: P3 necrotic leaf has become more desiccated; P2 specimen remains static but appears fragile.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of 'Stagnant Decline'. The lack of new growth and the presence of necrotic tissue suggest a nutrient deficiency or root-zone stress. The plants are surviving but not thriving under current lighting conditions.
vision_report.anomalies: Presence of white particulate matter (perlite or crushed eggshell) in soil surface; absence of P4.
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit reveals a high-stress environment. The Pothos (P3) is struggling with leaf necrosis, likely due to light-to-moisture imbalance. The Mexican Mint (P2) is reduced to a single, vulnerable leaf. The overall biome lacks vigor, and the 'Silver Guest' (P4) has likely succumbed to the environment.
